title: Triage 10 candidate cohorts through the gate
context: With the gate checklist (gate-003), NC policy (policy-nc-034), and pilot record (pilot-007) in place, M1 calls for triaging 10 distinct candidate cohorts through the gate to build the first credible set of PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE dispositions. Candidate cohorts are drawn from the candidate shortlist (outreach-006) and include seeds from cBioPortal public studies (which mirror cohorts hosted elsewhere — apply the identity rule), GDC non-TCGA open programs, GEO, EMBL-EBI, DepMap, GENIE, and aggregate stats sources. COSMIC and OncoKB are routed per policy-nc-034. Every REGISTERED-OPEN or mixed-tier candidate requires a mandatory second Access/License/PII sign-off. Controlled/identifiable candidates are EXCLUDED and routed to the excluded-resources register.
objective: Apply the gate-003 checklist to 10 distinct candidate cohorts (deduped per the identity rule) from the outreach-006 shortlist, producing a committed gate artifact (PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE) for each, with mandatory second sign-off on every REGISTERED-OPEN or mixed-tier candidate, ready to feed the M1 indexing tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Ten distinct cohorts (deduped per the cross-repository identity rule — a cohort in multiple portals counts once) are evaluated; each has a committed gate artifact (gates/<cohort-id>.json) recording PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE with the checks that ran and what fired.
Each PASS artifact records: license id/URL/snapshot reference, each rights boolean with a cited clause, access tier, PII check result, and reviewer sign-off.
Each FLAG/EXCLUDE artifact records the disqualifying reason: controlled tier, undocumented de-identification, NC terms (routed to policy-nc-034), or other.
Every REGISTERED-OPEN or mixed-tier candidate has a mandatory second independent Access/License/PII sign-off (100%, not sampled) recorded in the gate artifact.
Any controlled/identifiable candidate is EXCLUDED and a pointer entry is added to the excluded-resources register (exclude-005); no controlled resource is given a record.
cBioPortal public studies in the candidate pool are deduplicated against their upstream sources (GDC, Synapse, etc.) per the identity rule; the dedup decision is noted in the gate artifact.
COSMIC and OncoKB are dispositioned per policy-nc-034; their gate artifacts record the policy reference.
Output (ten gate artifacts + a triage summary document) licensed CC-BY-4.0; DCO signed-off.
output: Ten committed gate artifacts (gates/<cohort-id>.json) with PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE dispositions and a triage summary document, ready to feed the M1 indexing tasks (index-010, index-011).
